campaigns.context: AsyncRAT and DCrat payloads communicate via C2 infrastructure spread across multiple free DDNS providers including dynuddns.com, bumbleshrimp.com, ddnsguru.com, and 1cooldns.com, alongside a shared listener at 157.10.53.213 on ports 443 and 8080. A World Cup 2026-themed lure domain (keobongdaworldcup2026.com) is also present, consistent with social engineering timed to high-traffic sports events. Two researchers reported 93 IOCs between July 20 and July 21.
campaigns.name: AsyncRAT and DCrat C2 cluster abusing free DDNS providers

campaigns.context: A ClickFix campaign delivers Vidar stealer payloads via staged batch scripts, ZIP archives, and HTA files. Victims execute clipboard-injected commands after interacting with a fake browser verification page; payload files follow consistent naming conventions including N.zip, N_drv.bat, N_smb.bat, and N_ftp.bat. Infrastructure includes shopleta.ru and panychurasc0.com; six researchers reported 79 IOCs between July 18 and July 22.
campaigns.name: ClickFix AiTM phishing via Russian-domain infrastructure

campaigns.context: An Android malware cluster uses duckdns.org dynamic DNS subdomains and attacker-controlled GitHub Pages (github.io) alongside .top domains for command-and-control communication. Multiple MD5 and SHA-256 hashes indicate several associated samples in active circulation. Three researchers reported 20 IOCs between July 17 and July 22.
campaigns.name: Android malware cluster using duckdns and GitHub Pages for C2

campaigns.context: A single domain, lestrani.de, hosts subdomains impersonating at least five European organizations: Agenzia delle Entrate (Italian tax authority), ANTAI amendes (French road fines authority), Elster/Finanzamt (German tax portal), Commerzbank (German bank), and AXA insurance. The multi-country, multi-brand abuse of a single base domain is consistent with a phishing kit deployed by one actor or service running parallel credential-harvest pages for European victims across different national contexts. One researcher reported 18 IOCs on July 20.
campaigns.name: Multi-brand European gov and bank phishing on lestrani.de

campaigns.context: Multiple attacker-controlled Cloudflare Tunnel subdomains expose WsgiDAV or generic open directory listings used to stage and serve malware. The use of trycloudflare.com provides HTTPS coverage and bypasses domain-reputation controls by routing through trusted Cloudflare infrastructure. Three reporters flagged 15 combined IOCs across six tunnel hostnames between July 17 and July 22.
campaigns.name: Malware staging via WsgiDAV open directories on trycloudflare tunnels

campaigns.context: Attackers deliver ConnectWise ScreenConnect client MSI installers via legitimate cloud relay subdomains (rtsinternationals.screenconnect.com, gooseberry.screenconnect.com) and attacker-controlled domains including lockalcontractiongesatate.cc and a compromised WordPress site at ijexgroup.com.br. The MSI installer delivers persistent remote access under the guise of legitimate remote support software. Three reporters flagged 13 combined IOCs between July 17 and July 22.
campaigns.name: ScreenConnect remote access C2 across multiple lure domains

campaigns.context: A Magecart campaign uses relay subdomains on cloudservicecon.com, cdn-statmetrics.com, dinkfoundry.com, and infolservice.com to exfiltrate payment card data from compromised e-commerce sites, with faketools.click also associated with the cluster. The relay naming mimics legitimate CDN and analytics services to blend into normal web traffic. Two researchers reported 10 IOCs on July 22.
campaigns.name: Magecart payment skimming relay infrastructure

campaigns.context: Phishing pages impersonating Resona Bank appear on a subdomain of aochz.com alongside other domains serving credential-harvesting endpoints at consistent /login paths. The shared path pattern and subdomain naming across multiple hosts suggest a single kit operator; aochz.com previously appeared in a related Japanese consumer phishing cluster. Four researchers reported 9 IOCs between July 17 and July 22.
campaigns.name: Japanese bank phishing targeting Resona Bank via /login pages

campaigns.context: Two domains impersonating Tropical Smoothie Cafe - tropicalsmotiecafe.com and order-tropicalismoothecaffe.com - host order-themed credential-harvesting pages, with the latter also carrying a redundant order-prefix subdomain. Both were reported by the same researcher on July 21, suggesting a single actor registering multiple typosquat variants simultaneously. One researcher reported 8 combined IOCs on July 21.
campaigns.name: Tropical Smoothie Cafe brand phishing via typosquat domains

campaigns.context: The domain eznamsk.com hosts a phishing endpoint at the /sk/ path alongside a matomo.eznamsk.com analytics subdomain, indicating active monitoring of victim visits. The combination of a regional path identifier and analytics integration is consistent with a targeted credential-harvesting operation tracking visitor behavior. Two researchers reported 8 IOCs between July 21 and July 22.
campaigns.name: Credential phishing with analytics tracking at eznamsk.com

campaigns.context: Four domains - skjwoodstone.com, tonghedianzi.com, yijiatuliao.com, and zhuodashufa.com - all host phishing pages at /safety/update/, consistent with a single kit deployed across multiple hosts using a shared path as a campaign marker. Domain names appear to be transliterations of Chinese business terms, suggesting Chinese-registered infrastructure. One researcher reported 8 IOCs on July 22.
campaigns.name: Safety-page phishing kit deployed across four .com domains

campaigns.context: Remcos RAT is delivered via an HTA file with an unusually long filename designed to obscure the payload type, staged on an IP-based host and distributed via redirect URLs on fasthouse.me. Supporting payload URLs on zynimg.com and usafansweepstakes2026.us serve additional stages under image-hosting-style paths. One researcher reported 7 IOCs on July 21.
campaigns.name: Remcos RAT delivery via HTA loader with image-host staging

campaigns.context: The domain reha-stims.com hosts subdomains impersonating webmail portals for mzkt.by (a Belarusian heavy vehicle manufacturer) and customs.go.kr (South Korean customs authority). The multi-country, cross-sector targeting on a single base domain suggests a shared phishing service or kit operator running parallel credential-harvesting pages. One researcher reported 6 IOCs on July 22.
campaigns.name: Cross-country webmail phishing on reha-stims.com

campaigns.context: Two domains, 51bxun.com and cqurg.com, host phishing pages under a shared path /JxBgwINm, a pattern consistent with a single phishing kit deployment using a fixed campaign identifier across multiple hosts. The targeted brand or sector is not identifiable from the available IOCs. One researcher flagged 4 IOCs between July 17 and July 19.
campaigns.name: Phishing kit deployment on .com pair with fixed campaign path

campaigns.context: A subdomain of assistenza-spa.com named 'in-attesa-della-conferma-di-nexi' (Italian for 'waiting for Nexi confirmation') hosts a credential-harvesting page impersonating Nexi, Italy's leading payment processing company. The Italian-language subdomain targets consumers awaiting transaction approval notifications. One researcher reported 4 IOCs on July 21.
campaigns.name: Nexi payment phishing via assistenza-spa.com subdomain

campaigns.context: A domain named extensionscover.xyz serves a NetLock RMM Agent installer for 64-bit Windows alongside a PowerShell setup script, following a pattern consistent with malicious RMM tool deployment for unauthorized remote access. The domain name is designed to suggest browser extension infrastructure, likely as a social engineering lure to prompt installation. Two researchers flagged 4 IOCs on July 20.
campaigns.name: NetLock RMM agent delivery via extensionscover.xyz

campaigns.context: Sliver C2 implants beacon to 185.132.53.171 and 38.47.198.41, with the latter exposing listener ports 8082 and 8444. Four SHA-256 hashes indicate staged payload delivery consistent with an open directory on the C2 host. Two researchers reported 8 IOCs between July 15 and July 17.
campaigns.name: Sliver C2 framework on two IPs with open directory

campaigns.context: An Njrat C2 node at 193.161.193.99 is mapped through a portmap.host dynamic DNS subdomain that encodes an attacker handle and port number in the hostname (ahmeddoserr-37232). Portmap.host is a free port-forwarding service frequently abused by RAT operators to obscure static IP attribution and maintain C2 availability across IP changes. One researcher reported 4 IOCs on July 20.
campaigns.name: Njrat C2 via portmap.host dynamic DNS tunnel
